text: The database latency increased after deployment due to missing index.
The database latency increased after deployment due to missing index.
The database latency increased after deployment due to missing index.

The system architecture consists of a microservices-based backend with a central API gateway.

The Order Service handles all order-related operations, including creation, validation, and processing. It communicates with the Inventory Service and Payment Service.

During the latest deployment, a missing database index caused significant performance degradation in the Order Service. This resulted in slow queries and increased latency across the system.

The caching layer uses Redis, but cach

e invalidation strategies were not properly configured. This led to a high cache miss rate and increased database load.

To mitigate these issues, the team initiated a rollback and later implemented proper indexing and caching strategies.

Future improvements include:
- better observability
- automated performance testing
- improved deployment validation

[2025-02-10 10:15:10] WARNING: Missing index on table 'orders'
[2025-02-10 10:16:02] INFO: API server started on port 8080
[2025-02-10 10:18:33] ERROR: Query execution time exceeded threshold (1200ms)
[2025-02-10 10:19:10] INFO: Increased database CPU usage detected (85%)
[2025-02-10 10:20:44] WARNING: Slow query detected on endpoint /api/orders
[2025-02-10 10:22:01] E

RROR: Timeout error in service OrderProcessor
[2025-02-10 10:25:15] INFO: Cache miss rate increased to 65%
[2025-02-10 10:27:42] WARNING: Redis cache eviction rate high
[2025-02-10 10:30:00] ERROR: Latency spike detected (p95 = 1800ms)
[2025-02-10 10:32:11] INFO: Deployment rollback initiated
[2025-02-10 10:35:50] INFO: System stabilizing after rollback
[2025-02-10 10:40:00] INFO: Performance metrics returning to normal levels
[2025-02-10 10:45:30] INFO: Post-mortem analysis scheduled for
